Cambrian OCR — 3-tier Recaption Examples

Recaptioned by Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct, conditioned on image + original QA facts. ~150K samples across 5 datasets. Source: /weka/oe-training-default/oe-encoder/cambrian_ocr/recap/
8 random samples per dataset · seed=42

cambrian_arxivqa (44,026 samples)

ArXiv QA — scientific paper figures (LaTeX, plots, equations)
arxivqa_16447b325330
High-level (136 chars)
A scatter plot comparing LyC escape fractions across different galaxy groups, with star symbols indicating the broadest range of values.
Mid-level (690 chars)
The graph plots LyC Escape Fraction f_esc on the vertical axis against the ratio O32 = [OIII]5007,4959/[OII]3727 on the horizontal axis. Data points are categorized by symbol: red circles for LyC-LAEs at z=3 (this work), black circles for Ion2 (z=3.2; Vanzella+16,19), black stars for Low-z strong LyC leakers (Izotov+16a,b,18a,b), and black squares for Low-z LyC leakers (compiled by Izotov+18b). Two correlation lines are overlaid: a dashed line for Faisst16 and a solid line for Izotov+18a. The star symbols, representing Low-z strong LyC leakers, span the widest vertical range, from near zero to over 0.6, while the red circles cluster at lower values and the squares remain near zero.
Low-level (1065 chars)
[click to expand]
The chart’s vertical axis, labeled “LyC Escape Fraction f_esc,” ranges from 0 to 0.8, with major gridlines at 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, and 0.8. The horizontal axis is labeled “O32 = [OIII]5007,4959/[OII]3727,” with a logarithmic scale marked at 1 and 10. The legend in the upper-left corner defines each data series: red circles for LyC-LAEs at z=3 (this work), black circles for Ion2 (z=3.2; Vanzella+16,19), black stars for Low-z strong LyC leakers (Izotov+16a,b,18a,b), and black squares for Low-z LyC leakers (compiled by Izotov+18b). Two correlation lines are plotted: a dashed line labeled “Correlation by Faisst16” and a solid line labeled “Correlation by Izotov+18a.” The star symbols, representing Low-z strong LyC leakers, are scattered across the full vertical range, with one point near 0.7 and others near 0.1–0.3, indicating the largest spread in LyC escape fractions. The red circles are clustered between 0.2 and 0.4, while the black squares remain near zero. All data points are marked with error bars, and some have arrows pointing to the correlation lines.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- Which group of galaxies has the largest range of observed LyC escape fractions according to the graph? A) LyC-LAEs at z=3 (This work) B) Ion2 (z=3.2; Vanzella+16,19) C) Low-z strong LyC leakers (Izotov+16a,b,18a,b) D) Low-z LyC leakers (compiled by Izotov+18b): The group represented by stars, which corresponds to "Low-z strong LyC leakers (Izotov+16a,b,18a,b)", shows the widest range of LyC escape fractions, from nearly 0 to over 0.6. Therefore, the correct answer is C.
arxivqa_99131509178f
High-level (123 chars)
A diagram contrasting a multi-stage mesh regression pipeline with a proposed one-stage ROMP method for multi-person scenes.
Mid-level (717 chars)
The top section, labeled “Multi-stage Method,” shows a soccer scene with dashed outlines around players, illustrating challenges in multi-person detection. Below, section (b) depicts an existing pipeline: detection leads to single-person mesh regression, with a red dashed circle highlighting a problem area where two players are occluded. Section (c), labeled “Proposed one-stage pipeline,” introduces ROMP, which uses a heatmap and parameter sampling to directly generate a mesh for multiple people in one stage, bypassing the need for separate detection and single-person regression steps. The diagram visually emphasizes the transition from a fragmented, multi-stage process to a unified, one-stage ROMP approach.
Low-level (887 chars)
[click to expand]
The image is divided into three labeled sections. The top section, “Multi-stage Method,” displays a soccer scene with players outlined in dashed red and blue lines, indicating detection challenges. Below it, section (b) “Existing multi-stage pipeline” shows a flow: an input image of two players leads to “Detection,” then to a “Single-person mesh regression” step, with a red dashed circle around an occluded player pair. The bottom section, (c) “Proposed one-stage pipeline,” features a vertical “ROMP” block on the left, feeding into a “Body Center heatmap” and “Mesh Parameter map” with “Parameter sampling” indicated by yellow arrows. This outputs a single mesh of two players, visually demonstrating the consolidation of steps into one stage. The entire diagram uses consistent soccer imagery, white and blue tones, and clear directional arrows to contrast the old and new methods.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What is the primary focus of the proposed ROMP method as illustrated in the figure? A. It focuses on improving the accuracy of ball detection in sports scenes. B. It aims to enhance the detection of individual players in a multi-person scene. C. It is centered around the heatmap generation for mesh parameters in a single-person scene. D. It intends to streamline the mesh regression process into a one-stage pipeline.: The figure illustrates the transition from a multi-stage pipeline to a proposed one-stage pipeline for mesh regression, indicating a focus on streamlining the process. Therefore, the correct answer is D.
arxivqa_d39affe712d1
High-level (114 chars)
A five-panel scientific plot showing oscillatory behavior and spectral response under varying detuning conditions.
Mid-level (495 chars)
The image displays five labeled subplots: (a) and (b) show log-scale plots of ⟨εs,j⟩ and ⟨εr,j⟩ versus Ω₀tₛ/2π, with three colored curves for j=0,1,2. Plots (c), (d), and (e) show |F[s(t)]|² versus Ω/Ω₀, each with distinct detuning conditions. Plot (c) has δΩ=0 and t_w=2.5×(2π/Ω₀), (d) has δΩ=0 and t_w=2×(2π/Ω₀), and (e) has δΩ≠0 and t_w=2×(2π/Ω₀). In (e), the nonzero detuning visibly shifts the spectral peaks to the right compared to (d), confirming a rightward shift in response frequency.
Low-level (843 chars)
[click to expand]
The image contains five distinct plots arranged in two columns. The left column features plots (a) and (b), both with logarithmic y-axes and x-axes labeled Ω₀tₛ/2π, ranging from 10⁻¹ to 10⁰. Plot (a) shows ⟨εs,j⟩ with three curves: green for j=0, magenta for j=1, and blue for j=2. Plot (b) shows ⟨εr,j⟩ with the same color coding. The right column contains plots (c), (d), and (e), each with x-axis labeled Ω/Ω₀ from 0 to 2 and y-axis labeled |F[s(t)]|² from 0 to 1. Plot (c) is labeled with t_w = 2.5×(2π/Ω₀) and δΩ=0; plot (d) with t_w = 2×(2π/Ω₀) and δΩ=0; plot (e) with t_w = 2×(2π/Ω₀) and δΩ≠0. In plot (e), the spectral peaks are visibly shifted to the right relative to plot (d), indicating that nonzero detuning alters the resonance frequency. All plots use thin black gridlines, and the curves are colored consistently across panels.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- In figure (e), what effect does the nonzero detuning \(\delta\Omega\) have on the function \(|F(s(t))|^2\)? A) It increases the maximum amplitude of \(|F(s(t))|^2\). B) It shifts the peak of \(|F(s(t))|^2\) to the right. C) It has no effect on the shape or position of \(|F(s(t))|^2\). D) It creates additional peaks in \(|F(s(t))|^2\).: In figure (e), the introduction of a nonzero detuning \(\delta\Omega\) appears to shift the peak of the function \(|F(s(t))|^2\) to the right compared to the case when \(\delta\Omega = 0\). This is indicative of the effect of detuning in oscillatory systems, where the resonance condition is altered, leading to a shift in the response frequency. Therefore, the correct answer is B.
arxivqa_2e13b128c72f
High-level (146 chars)
A log-log plot showing clustering strength ξ(r) versus scale r (Mpc) for three astronomical object groups, with distinct symbols and model curves.
Mid-level (589 chars)
The graph plots ξ(r) on the y-axis against r (Mpc) on the x-axis, both on logarithmic scales. Three data series are shown: blue stars for EAGLE LAEs, green triangles for GALICS LAEs, and red circles for URCHIN DLAs, each with error bars. Three dashed lines represent theoretical models for r₀ = 2, 3.6, and 5 cMpc. At small scales (<1 Mpc), the EAGLE LAEs (blue stars) are positioned highest on the y-axis, indicating the strongest clustering. At 10 Mpc, the URCHIN DLAs (red circles) are positioned higher than the EAGLE LAEs, indicating stronger clustering for URCHIN DLAs at that scale.
Low-level (939 chars)
[click to expand]
The chart’s y-axis, labeled ξ(r), uses a logarithmic scale from 10⁻¹ to 10², while the x-axis, labeled r (Mpc), ranges from 10⁻¹ to 10¹. The legend box in the lower-left corner identifies the three data series: blue stars for EAGLE LAEs, green triangles for GALICS LAEs, and red circles for URCHIN DLAs. The legend also includes three dashed lines representing models with r₀ values of 2, 3.6, and 5 cMpc. The EAGLE LAEs data points are consistently above the others at scales less than 1 Mpc, indicating the highest clustering strength in that range. At 10 Mpc, the red circle data points for URCHIN DLAs are higher than the blue star points for EAGLE LAEs, indicating stronger clustering for URCHIN DLAs at that scale. The error bars for each data point are vertical, indicating uncertainty in the ξ(r) measurement. The plot background is white with black grid lines and axes, and all symbols are filled with color and outlined in black.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- Which group of objects shows the highest clustering strength at the smallest scales (< 1 Mpc) according to the graph? EAGLE LAEs GALICS LAEs URCHIN DLAs The graph shows no significant difference at scales < 1 Mpc -: Clustering strength on the graph is indicated by the value of ξ(r) at a given scale r (Mpc). At scales < 1 Mpc, the EAGLE LAEs show the highest values of ξ(r), indicating the strongest clustering at these scales compared to GALICS LAEs and URCHIN DLAs. Therefore, the correct answer is A. EAGLE LAEs.
- According to the data in the graph, how does the clustering strength for EAGLE LAEs compare to URCHIN DLAs at 10 Mpc? EAGLE LAEs have stronger clustering than URCHIN DLAs at 10 Mpc. URCHIN DLAs have stronger clustering than EAGLE LAEs at 10 Mpc. EAGLE LAEs and URCHIN DLAs have the same clustering strength at 10 Mpc. The graph does not provide information for 10 Mpc.: At 10 Mpc, the ξ(r) value for URCHIN DLAs is higher than that for EAGLE LAEs, as shown by the position of the respective data points relative to each other. This indicates stronger clustering for URCHIN DLAs at this scale. Therefore, the correct answer is B. URCHIN DLAs have stronger clustering than EAGLE LAEs at 10 Mpc..
arxivqa_ebfac8a9b6d4
High-level (170 chars)
A composite figure illustrating acoustic wave-induced strain in a semiconductor, with strain visualization, band structure, and stress-dependent refractive index changes.
Mid-level (763 chars)
The top panel (a) displays a color-coded vector field with arrows indicating strain direction and magnitude, labeled "tension" on the left and "compression" on the right, showing alternating regions of mechanical deformation. Below it, a band structure diagram plots energy versus position, with conduction band (CB) and valence band (VB) curves, and arrows indicating photon absorption (hv) and bandgap energy transitions (E_max_g, E_min_g). The lower section (b) contains a graph of Δn_eff versus h/H, with labeled points A, B, C, alongside three thermal stress maps showing strain distribution under those conditions. The rightmost panel (c) plots Δn_eff versus the square root of power density, with a legend specifying h = 200 nm, H = 500 nm, and W = 600 nm.
Low-level (1081 chars)
[click to expand]
The top panel (a) features a vector field with red arrows concentrated in tension zones and blue arrows in compression zones, overlaid on a color gradient from yellow to cyan, with a coordinate system (x, y, z) at the lower left. Below it, a band structure plot shows energy (a.u.) on the vertical axis and position (x, λ_SAW) on the horizontal axis, with solid and dotted curves labeled CB and VB, and arrows indicating photon energy (hv) and bandgap energies (E_max_g, E_min_g). The lower-left panel (b) contains a line graph of Δn_eff [x10⁻⁴] versus h/H, with a blue curve peaking near h/H = 0.6, marked with points A, B, C. Adjacent to it are three thermal stress maps labeled A, B, C, each showing a color-coded stress distribution (blue to red) with a vertical colorbar labeled "Max stress." The lower-right panel (c) is a scatter plot of Δn_eff [x10⁻⁴] versus √Pℓ [W/m], with green dots forming a nearly linear trend, and a legend box specifying h = 200 nm, H = 500 nm, W = 600 nm. All panels are framed with black borders and use consistent font styles for labels and axes.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What phenomenon is depicted in the top part of the figure (a)? A) Diffraction of light in a prism B) Displacement of molecules in a liquid C) Acoustic waves inducing strain in a semiconductor D) Magnetic field lines around a conductor: The top part of figure (a) shows an array of arrows indicating direction and magnitude of movement, characteristic of waves, labeled with "tension" and "compression," which are terms associated with acoustic waves. The strain in a semiconductor is depicted by the pattern of arrows showing the alternating areas of tension and compression typical of an acoustic wave's effect on a material. Therefore, the correct answer is C) Acoustic waves inducing strain in a semiconductor.
arxivqa_10a47cceb520
High-level (106 chars)
A log-log scatter plot showing a power-law relationship between average galaxy cluster mass and halo mass.
Mid-level (568 chars)
The graph displays a log-log scale with the y-axis labeled ⟨N⟩/M and the x-axis labeled M/h⁻¹M_⊙, both spanning several orders of magnitude. A solid diagonal trend line passes through a cluster of larger, open-circle data points, indicating a direct linear relationship in logarithmic space. This line suggests a power-law scaling between the two variables. The background is filled with a dense field of smaller, fainter dots, representing additional data points. The trend line’s slope is positive and consistent, confirming the power-law nature of the relationship.
Low-level (860 chars)
[click to expand]
The vertical y-axis is labeled ⟨N⟩/M and is scaled logarithmically from 10⁰ to 10², with tick marks at 10⁰, 10¹, and 10². The horizontal x-axis is labeled M/h⁻¹M_⊙ and spans from 10¹² to 10¹⁵, with major grid lines at 10¹³, 10¹⁴, and 10¹⁵. A solid black line, representing the trend, runs diagonally from the lower-left to the upper-right, passing through several large open-circle data points. These points are positioned along the line, indicating a direct linear relationship on the log-log scale. The background is densely populated with small, faint dots, likely representing individual measurements or simulations, scattered across the plot area. The axes are framed by thin black lines, and the grid lines are dashed, aiding in reading the logarithmic scale. The overall layout is clean and scientific, with no additional annotations or legends visible.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What does the trend line in the graph suggest about the relationship between average galaxy cluster mass ⟨N⟩/M and halo mass M/h⁻¹M_⊙? There is an exponential relationship between ⟨N⟩/M and M/h⁻¹M_⊙. The relationship between ⟨N⟩/M and M/h⁻¹M_⊙ is inversely proportional. There is a direct linear relationship between ⟨N⟩/M and M/h⁻¹M_⊙. ⟨N⟩/M remains constant regardless of the changes in M/h⁻¹M_⊙.: The trend line on the graph shows a straight line on a log-log scale, indicating a power-law relationship, which is a type of direct linear relationship in logarithmic terms. Therefore, the correct answer is C.
arxivqa_946addc95d40
High-level (125 chars)
A grouped bar chart comparing three encoding types across binary-coded semantic categories, with a red dashed threshold line.
Mid-level (826 chars)
The chart displays outcome probabilities on the vertical axis, ranging from 0.00 to 0.20, with a horizontal red dashed line positioned at approximately 0.125, serving as a significance threshold. The horizontal axis lists semantic categories encoded as strings like “ADULT,SIT,INSIDE |01100⟩” and “CHILD,MOVE,INSIDE |10111⟩”, each with three vertical bars. Blue bars represent “meaning space,” orange bars “encoding (ADULT,STAND,INSIDE)”, and green bars “encoding (CHILD,MOVE,INSIDE)”. Across all categories, the green bars are consistently taller than the orange bars, indicating that the “CHILD,MOVE,INSIDE” encodings generally yield higher outcome probabilities than the “ADULT,STAND,INSIDE” encodings. The tallest bar overall is green, corresponding to “SURGEON,MOVE,OUTSIDE |10111⟩”, which exceeds the red threshold line.
Low-level (1185 chars)
[click to expand]
The chart’s title is absent, but its legend in the top right corner clearly labels the three bar types: blue for “meaning space”, orange for “encoding (ADULT,STAND,INSIDE)”, and green for “encoding (CHILD,MOVE,INSIDE)”. The vertical axis is labeled “Outcome prob.” with tick marks at 0.00, 0.05, 0.10, 0.15, and 0.20. A red dashed line cuts horizontally across the chart at approximately 0.125, visually separating categories with higher from lower probabilities. The x-axis contains eight distinct category labels, each with three bars, including “ADULT,SIT,INSIDE |01100⟩”, “ADULT,SLEEP,INSIDE |01000⟩”, “SMITH,SIT,INSIDE |01110⟩”, “SMITH,SLEEP,INSIDE |01101⟩”, “CHILD,STAND,OUTSIDE |10011⟩”, “CHILD,MOVE,OUTSIDE |10111⟩”, “SURGEON,STAND,OUTSIDE |10001⟩”, and “SURGEON,MOVE,OUTSIDE |10101⟩”. The orange bars for “ADULT,STAND,INSIDE” are consistently shorter than the green bars for “CHILD,MOVE,INSIDE” in every group, confirming that the latter encodings generally produce higher outcome probabilities. The tallest bar is green, for “SURGEON,MOVE,OUTSIDE |10111⟩”, which exceeds the red threshold line. The chart’s background is white, and all bars are separated by thin white space.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What does the dashed red line across the graph most likely represent? A. The average outcome probability across all categories. B. The threshold for a significant outcome probability. C. The maximum outcome probability recorded in the graph. D. The boundary between inside and outside encodings. -: The dashed red line across the graph is at a constant level above all the bars, suggesting it is a threshold line that indicates a significant level of outcome probability that the categories are being compared against. Therefore, the correct answer is B.
- Based on the graph, what can be inferred about the encodings for 'CHILD,MOVE,INSIDE'? A. They are generally lower than the encodings for 'ADULT,STAND,INSIDE'. B. They peak at 'CHILD_STAND_OUTSIDE [10111]'. C. They have a constant value across all categories. D. They are highest for the 'ADULT_SIT_INSIDE [01100]' category.: The orange bars representing 'CHILD,MOVE,INSIDE' are generally lower than the green bars for 'ADULT,STAND,INSIDE' across all categories, which indicates that the encodings for 'CHILD,MOVE,INSIDE' are generally lower. Therefore, the correct answer is A.
arxivqa_a3198af043b7
High-level (113 chars)
Four panels display temporal intensity, power spectral density, and mode number distributions for AS and S modes.
Mid-level (722 chars)
Panel (a) plots intensity (P_int) over slow time (nsec), with the green AS mode line consistently above the purple S mode line, indicating higher intensity for AS throughout. Panel (b) shows the power spectral density (PSD) in dB, with both modes exhibiting overlapping, jagged peaks that rise and fall together. Panels (c) and (d) are spectrograms showing slow frequency (GHz) versus mode number, with both AS and S modes symmetrically distributed around mode number zero, marked by white dots and vertical lines. Panel (c) labels the AS mode and includes a Δω symbol between two vertical lines, indicating the frequency shift between the two modes. Panel (d) labels the S mode and shows a similar symmetric distribution.
Low-level (1057 chars)
[click to expand]
The figure is divided into four labeled panels (a, b, c, d) arranged vertically. Panel (a) has a y-axis labeled "P_int (a.u.)" and x-axis "Slow time (nsec)", with a legend indicating green as "AS mode" and purple as "S mode". The green line remains above the purple line across the entire time range, showing higher intensity for AS. Panel (b) has a y-axis labeled "PSD (dB)" and x-axis unlabeled but implied to be slow time, with both lines showing similar, overlapping, jagged peaks. Panels (c) and (d) are spectrograms with y-axis "Slow frequency (GHz)" and x-axis "Mode number". Panel (c) is labeled "AS mode" and features a Δω symbol between two vertical lines, indicating a frequency difference. Panel (d) is labeled "S mode" and shows a symmetric distribution of white dots around mode number zero. Both panels (c) and (d) display a blue gradient background with horizontal striations, and the mode number axis ranges from -200 to 200. The overall layout is clean, with clear axis labels and legends, and the panels are separated by thin white lines.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What does panel (a) in the figure indicate about the AS and S modes? The AS mode has a higher intensity over slow time than the S mode. The S mode dominates the AS mode throughout the entire slow time. The AS and S modes have identical intensities over the slow time. The AS mode shows more variability in intensity over slow time compared to the S mode.: Panel (a) shows two lines representing the intensity (P_int) over a period of slow time (nsec). The AS mode, represented by the green line, is consistently above the S mode, represented by the purple line, indicating it has a higher intensity throughout the observed time. Therefore, the correct answer is A.
- Based on panels (c) and (d), how do the AS and S modes compare in terms of mode number distribution? Both modes are symmetrically distributed around the mode number zero. AS mode is distributed more towards the positive mode numbers compared to the S mode. S mode is distributed more towards negative mode numbers compared to the AS mode. AS mode has a wider spread in mode number distribution than the S mode.: In panels (c) and (d), both AS mode and S mode show distributions along the mode number axis that are symmetric around zero. There is no clear shift towards positive or negative mode numbers for either mode, and neither shows a wider spread than the other. Therefore, the correct answer is A.
- What does the Δω symbol in panel (c) signify? The change in power spectral density (PSD) between modes. The frequency shift between AS and S modes. The power output difference in gigahertz over slow time. The time interval between the occurrence of the AS and S modes.: In the context of the figure, Δω generally represents a frequency difference. Panel (c) is a spectrogram showing frequency over slow time, and the Δω symbol is placed between two vertical lines indicating two separate modes, suggesting it represents the frequency shift between AS and S modes. Therefore, the correct answer is B.

cambrian_ocr_vqa (61,250 samples)

OCR-VQA — book/document cover OCR + visual QA
ocr_vqa_073c5eed7474
High-level (89 chars)
A framed, large-scale 3D topographical map of Israel, highlighting terrain and key sites.
Mid-level (619 chars)
The image displays a framed, vertically oriented 3D relief map of Israel, enclosed in a light wood-colored frame with a white mat. The title "Israel" is printed in bold black at the top left. The map uses color gradients and raised terrain to depict topography: green for coastal plains, brown for central hills, and white for mountainous regions. A small inset legend is visible in the lower right corner, and a blue Mediterranean Sea is shown along the western edge. The map’s scale is indicated as 1:1,000,000. The map is placed on a tiled floor, and its large size is evident from the visible frame and perspective.
Low-level (873 chars)
[click to expand]
The map is a raised relief 3D representation of Israel, framed in light wood with a white mat border. The title "Israel" is printed in bold black at the top left corner. The terrain is rendered in three dimensions, with varying elevations and textures: green for lowlands, brown for central hills, and white for highlands, including the Golan Heights and the Dead Sea region. The Mediterranean Sea is shown in blue along the western edge, with a small inset legend in the lower right corner. The scale is marked as 1:1,000,000. The map’s dimensions are indicated as 20" x 9" in the legend. The map is mounted on a tiled floor, and its large size is evident from the visible frame and perspective. The map is clearly intended for travel use, as it highlights geographical features and sites of interest, though no specific Christian sites are labeled in the visible portion.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What is the title of this book?
Answer the question using a single word or phrase.: Raised Relief 3D Map of Israel (LARGE: 20" x 9") with Sites of Christian Interest
- What is the genre of this book?: Travel
- Is this book related to Travel?: Yes
- Is this book related to Arts & Photography?: No

High-level (104 chars)
A book cover for a test preparation guide to private schools in New York, authored by Catherine Hausman.
Mid-level (653 chars)
The cover features the title “The Manhattan Family Guide to Private Schools” in large, bold, black text within a white rectangular box, with a subtitle “69+ elementary and high schools in the Greater New York area” below it. The authors’ names, “Catherine Hausman and Victoria Goldman,” are printed at the bottom in black. A circular badge in the top right corner indicates the book is “Revised and Expanded.” The background is a stylized map of Manhattan, rendered in muted green, beige, and blue tones, with small black dots representing school locations. The overall design is clean and functional, with a warm orange border framing the entire cover.
Low-level (919 chars)
[click to expand]
The cover’s layout is vertically structured, with the title block occupying the upper half, the author names at the bottom, and the map filling the central space. The title is presented in a bold, sans-serif font, with “The Manhattan Family Guide to Private Schools” in the largest size, followed by the subtitle in a smaller, lighter weight. The authors’ names are centered beneath the map, printed in a clean, readable typeface. The map of Manhattan is simplified, showing the island’s shape with water bodies in blue and landmasses in beige and green, with small black dots scattered across the land to indicate school locations. A thin orange border frames the entire cover, and a white circular badge in the top right corner contains the text “Revised and Expanded” in black. The overall color palette is warm and earthy, with no imagery suggesting humor or entertainment, aligning with its test preparation genre.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- Who wrote this book?
Answer the question using a single word or phrase.: Catherine Hausman
- What is the title of this book?: The Manhattan Family Guide to Private Schools: 68+ Elementary and High Schools in the Greater New York Area
- What is the genre of this book?: Test Preparation
- Is this book related to Test Preparation?: Yes

ocr_vqa_252f751cd586
High-level (143 chars)
A pixel-art book cover for “Tales of Ancient Egypt” by Roger Lancelyn Green, depicting an Egyptian warrior confronting a serpent near pyramids.
Mid-level (702 chars)
The cover features a pixelated illustration of a warrior in blue and white attire, wielding a spear, standing atop a fallen log in a desert landscape. To his left, a green serpent coils near a white skull, while a large brown pyramid dominates the left side of the composition. The background is a gradient of yellow and orange, suggesting a desert sunset. The title “TALES OF ANCIENT EGYPT” is displayed in large, white, blocky pixel font at the top, with the subtitle “(Puffin Pixels)” in smaller white text beneath it. The author’s name, “ROGER LANCELYN GREEN,” appears in white pixel font at the bottom. The overall aesthetic is retro video game style, consistent with the “Puffin Pixels” branding.

[click to expand]

The author’s name, “ROGER LANCELYN GREEN,” is centered at the bottom in white pixel font. The central illustration depicts a warrior in blue and white, holding a spear, standing on a fallen log. To the left, a green pixelated serpent coils near a white skull. A large brown pyramid with a brick-like texture occupies the left side of the frame. The background is a gradient of yellow and orange, evoking a desert sunset. The entire scene is rendered in a retro video game aesthetic with visible pixel blocks, and the composition is framed by a thin black border. The visual style and the “Puffin Pixels” branding suggest it is targeted toward a teen and young adult audience.

The mask’s surface shows visible wear, cracks, and discoloration, suggesting historical or ritual use. The background is a solid, dark, almost black color, which contrasts sharply with the mask and the white text, enhancing their visibility. The cover displays the title “El país bajo mi piel” in large green script, with the subtitle “MEMORIAS DE AMOR Y GUERRA” in smaller black uppercase letters. Below, the author’s name “GIOCONDA BELLi” is printed in bold red uppercase, followed by the phrase “AUTORA DEL BESTSELLER INTERNACIONAL LA MUJER HABITADA” in smaller black text. A quote from the Chicago Tribune appears at the bottom: “Un cuento poético, revelador y penetrante sobre el amor, la guerra, la literatura y la política… lírico, dramático e incisivo.” The background image is a black-and-white photograph of a woman seated, wearing a t-shirt with palm trees, holding a small object in one hand while gripping a rifle with the other. The composition is split horizontally, with the photograph occupying the top two-thirds and the text overlaying the lower third.

The image is monochromatic, with high contrast and a slightly grainy texture, suggesting a historical or documentary tone. Avant,” is centered below the subtitle in a medium-sized black font. On the lower-left, a grayscale photograph of a military helicopter in mid-flight is angled diagonally, partially overlapping a white, torn-paper graphic. This graphic contains a job advertisement reading: “320 Help Wanted MILITARY PERSONNEL Seeking candidates with military and/or law enforcement experience. Experience in any branch of Special Forces is particularly welcome. Individuals must be able to work and live in a hostile field environment for extended periods of time. Salary: competitive.” At the bottom center, the publisher’s name, “CAMBRIDGE,” is displayed in white uppercase letters within a small black rectangular box. The overall layout is clean, with strong visual hierarchy emphasizing the title and author, while the photographic and textual elements reinforce the book’s subject matter related to privatized security and military operations.

screen_qa_4625c4df49d7
High-level (99 chars)
A dark-themed app consent screen for "Photo Window" requiring age verification and EULA acceptance.
Mid-level (597 chars)
The screen displays a dark gray modal titled "Photo Window" with a warning about photosensitive seizures. It states that pressing 'Accept' confirms the user is at least 13 years old and agrees to the EULA. Below, the End-User License Agreement details consent for Google Analytics, advertising networks, and social network features, noting data sharing with partner services. A green link to the "PHOTO WINDOW PRIVACY POLICY" is present. At the bottom, "DECLINE" and "ACCEPT" buttons are centered, with "ACCEPT" in green. A small copyright notice for GIF and CompuServe appears at the very bottom.
Low-level (1117 chars)
[click to expand]
The screen has a black status bar at the top showing time (9:30) and app icons. The main content is a dark gray modal with white and green text. The title "Photo Window" is in bold white at the top. Below it, a warning reads: "Welcome to the Photo Window! Please make sure you read the following warning:" followed by a green heading "PHOTOSENSITIVE SEIZURE WARNING". The next section states: "By pressing the 'Accept' button you confirm, that you are at least 13 years old and you agree to our EULA terms:". Below that, "END-USER LICENSE AGREEMENT (EULA)" is in green, followed by text detailing consent for Google Analytics, advertising networks, and social features, with a note that such services may use device identifiers and share data. A green link "PHOTO WINDOW PRIVACY POLICY" is below this. At the bottom center are two buttons: "DECLINE" and "ACCEPT", both in white text, with "ACCEPT" highlighted in green. At the very bottom, a small white text reads: "The Graphics Interchange Format© is the Copyright property of CompuServe Incorporated. GIF(sm) is a Service Mark property of CompuServe Incorporated."
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What is the advantage of pressing 'Accept' button?: <no answer>
- What is the mentioned age?: The mentioned age is at least 13 years old.

screen_qa_6ae1997a08e6
High-level (91 chars)
A mobile screen displaying product reviews, with user names, timestamps, and feedback text.
Mid-level (931 chars)
The screen shows a list of five customer reviews under the header "Product Reviews." The top review is from Joëlle, who wrote it 1 month ago and said, "I use it as a spring coat too love it." Below that, Paxton’s review, posted 3 months ago, reads: "this jacket runs very small i recommend ordering it a size or 2 larger then normal." Swan’s review, from 5 months ago, states: "this is very useful, in summer i use it without fur as raincoat, when it's getting colder i put the fur in." Cheryl’s review, posted 1 year ago, says: "I love the was the coat fits excepts for the upper arms too tight on both inner fur liner & outer coat." The final visible review is from Stephanie, who wrote it 1 year ago, though her full text is cut off. Each review is preceded by a circular placeholder icon, except Stephanie’s, which shows a photo of a couple. The interface has a black status bar at the top and navigation buttons at the bottom.
Low-level (982 chars)
[click to expand]
The screen’s top bar displays "Product Reviews" in white text on a black background, with a back arrow on the left and a status bar showing time (11:54) and connectivity icons. Below, each review is separated by a thin horizontal line and consists of a circular avatar placeholder (except for Stephanie, which shows a photo of a smiling couple), the reviewer’s name in bold, a timestamp in gray text (e.g., “Wrote 1 month ago”), and the review text in black. Joëlle’s review is the most recent, posted 1 month ago, and praises the coat as a spring garment. Paxton’s review, from 3 months ago, advises sizing up. Swan’s review, from 5 months ago, describes seasonal use with and without fur. Cheryl’s review, from 1 year ago, expresses love for the fit except for tightness in the upper arms on both fur layers. Stephanie’s review, also from 1 year ago, is partially visible. The layout is vertically stacked, with consistent spacing and font sizes, and the background is light gray.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- How many months ago did Paxton post a review?: Paxton posted a review 3 months ago.
- What review did Cheryl had posted?: The review posted by Cheryl was "I love the was the coat fits excepts for the upper arms too tight on both inner fur liner &outer coat".
- Who wrote the review 1 month ago?: The review was written by Joëlle.

The image is dominated by a dark gray, subtly textured background that mimics the look of aged concrete or slate, providing a neutral, grounded visual field. Centered on this background is the main quote, rendered in a clean, sans-serif typeface. The first line, “The Universe doesn’t want you to,” is in white. The word “BUY” is emphasized in bold yellow, followed by “something.” in white. The second line, “It wants you to,” is white, followed by “BE” in bold yellow, and “something.” in white. Below the quote, the brand identifier “Truth Serum TM” is displayed in smaller white text, with “Serum” highlighted in yellow and a trademark symbol (™) in white. The entire composition is vertically aligned and centered, with generous spacing around the text, creating a focused, impactful visual that reinforces the message of inner development over material acquisition.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What is the message of the text in the image?: The message of the text in the image is that the Universe is more concerned with who you become and what kind of person you are, rather than the material possessions you acquire. The focus should be on personal growth, character, and inner development.
- Can you explain the meaning of "Truth Serum TM" in this context?: "Truth Serum TM" in this context implies that the message is a dose of honest wisdom or a genuine insight about life. It suggests that focusing on who you are is the truth that the Universe wants you to understand and embrace, rather than prioritizing material possessions.

A classical music album cover featuring Martha Argerich and Claudio Abbado with the Orchestra Mozart performing Mozart piano concertos.
Mid-level (619 chars)

The man, Claudio Abbado, is on the left, holding a baton, while the woman, Martha Argerich, is on the right, seated at a piano. The name “Mozart” is written in large, elegant gold cursive script across the left side. Below it, in smaller gold sans-serif text, are the names “MARTHA ARGERICH” and “CLAUDIO ABBADO,” followed by “ORCHESTRA MOZART.” At the bottom, also in gold, is the text “Piano Concertos K 503 & K 466.” In the top right corner, a yellow Deutsche Grammophon logo is visible.

The overall design is minimalist, with gold text contrasting against the black background and the performers’ dark clothing.

llavar_ea9bc1465a7f
High-level (112 chars)
A USDA report detailing dairy product prices and sales in the U.S., with data for the week ending March 5, 2016.
Mid-level (740 chars)
The document, titled “National Dairy Products Sales Report,” is issued by the United States Department of Agriculture’s Agricultural Marketing Service. It presents highlights and detailed tables for butter, cheddar cheese, dry whey, and nonfat dry milk. The report notes that butter prices for the week ending March 5, 2016, averaged $2.03 per pound for USDA Grade AA standards, a 4.1-cent decrease from the prior week. A table labeled “Butter Prices and Sales” shows weighted prices and sales figures for five dates, including 2.1704 and 4,502,912 for 6-Feb, with asterisks indicating revised data. The report also includes price and sales data for cheddar cheese, dry whey, and nonfat dry milk, with specific price changes noted for each.
Low-level (952 chars)
[click to expand]
The document’s header features the USDA logo and the title “National Dairy Products Sales Report” in bold, centered text, with “United States Department of Agriculture” and “March 9, 2016” below. The report is divided into two main sections: “National Dairy Products Sales Report Highlights” and “National Dairy Products Sales Report 2/6/2016 – 3/5/2016.” The highlights section details price changes for butter, cheddar cheese, dry whey, and nonfat dry milk, including specific figures like butter’s $2.03 per pound average and a 4.1-cent decrease. The second section contains a table titled “Butter Prices and Sales,” with columns for dates (6-Feb, 13-Feb, 20-Feb, 27-Feb, 5-Mar) and rows for “Weighted Price” and “Sales,” with values like $2.1704, $4,502,912, and asterisks denoting revised data. The text is formatted in a clean, professional layout with consistent font sizes and spacing, and all data is presented in a structured, tabular format.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What is the main content of the text in the image?: The text in the image is about the USDA Marketing National Dairy Products Sales Report, which provides information on dairy product prices and sales in the United States.
- What are some of the products mentioned in the report?: Some of the dairy products mentioned in the report include butter, cheddar cheese, dry whey, and nonfat dry milk.
- What happened to the butter prices for the week ending March 5, 2016?: For the week ending March 5, 2016, butter prices received for 25 kilogram and 68 pound boxes meeting USDA Grade AA standards averaged 2.03 dollars per pound. The US price per pound decreased by 4.1 cents from the previous week.

A book cover for "Feeding My Mother" by Jann Arden, featuring a photo of her and her mother with floral borders and a message about love and care.
Mid-level (630 chars)
The cover displays the author’s name, “JANN ARDEN,” in large orange uppercase letters at the top. Below, a centered photograph shows Jann Arden and her mother smiling in a kitchen. The title, “FEEDING MY MOTHER,” is printed in large white uppercase letters beneath the photo, followed by the subtitle, “COMFORT AND LAUGHTER IN THE KITCHEN AS MY MOM LIVES WITH MEMORY LOSS,” in smaller white text. The word “love” appears in lowercase white letters, centered at the bottom of the cover. Low-level (915 chars)
[click to expand]
The cover’s layout is vertically structured: “JANN ARDEN” in bold orange sans-serif font anchors the top. Centered below is a framed photograph of Jann Arden and her mother, both smiling, set against a kitchen backdrop with white cabinets and a countertop. Beneath the photo, the title “FEEDING MY MOTHER” is rendered in large, bold white sans-serif type, followed by the subtitle “COMFORT AND LAUGHTER IN THE KITCHEN AS MY MOM LIVES WITH MEMORY LOSS” in smaller white sans-serif font. At the bottom center, the word “love” appears in lowercase white letters, emphasizing emotional support. The left and right edges are adorned with ornate floral motifs featuring stylized red, orange, and green flowers and leaves on a dark blue background, creating a warm, intimate visual frame. The overall color palette combines soft blue, vibrant floral accents, and clean white text, conveying tenderness and domestic warmth.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What is the main topic of Jann Arden's book "Feeding My Mother"?: The main topic of Jann Arden's book "Feeding My Mother" is comfort and laughter in the kitchen as her mom lives with memory loss.
- What is the significance of the words 'Love' in the image?: The words 'love' in the image signify the affection, care, and support that Jann Arden provides to her mother while facing the challenges of memory loss. It emphasizes the importance of love in dealing with such difficult situations.

A vertically stacked magazine spread featuring black-and-white imagery and text, conveying a dark, mysterious mood under the title “Now It’s Dark.”
Mid-level (695 chars)
The layout consists of four stacked panels. The top panel features a close-up of a man in a suit smoking, with the title “NOW IT’S DARK” in bold white sans-serif text to the right, accompanied by a faint smoke trail. Below, a second panel shows a man in profile, looking down, with a block of italicized text on the left and a smaller photo of a woman’s face. The third panel is a two-column layout with dense text and a small inset photo of a woman’s face on the left, alongside a larger photo of a man smoking. The bottom panel displays another two-column text layout with a large photo of a man smoking on the right. All images are in black and white, reinforcing the somber, mysterious tone.
Low-level (957 chars)
[click to expand]
The topmost panel presents a dramatic black-and-white portrait of a man in a suit, holding a cigarette to his lips, with smoke curling upward. To the right, the title “NOW IT’S DARK” is rendered in large, bold, white sans-serif letters, with a faint, wispy smoke graphic extending from the text. Below, the second panel shows a man in profile, looking downward, with a block of italicized text on the left and a small inset photo of a woman’s face. The third panel is split into two columns: the left contains dense, justified text with a small photo of a woman’s face at the top, while the right features a larger photo of a man smoking. The bottom panel continues the two-column text layout, with the right side dominated by a large, close-up photo of a man smoking, his expression somber. All images are monochromatic, enhancing the dark, mysterious mood. The overall design uses high contrast, minimal color, and stark typography to emphasize the theme.
Raw QA facts (input)
[click to expand]
- What is the mood conveyed by the magazine spread?: The mood conveyed by the magazine spread is dark and mysterious, with the black and white images of the man and woman emphasizing the theme "Now It's Dark."

The object is clearly identifiable as a cup, featuring a cylindrical body with a slightly flared rim and a C-shaped handle attached to its right side. A handle extends from the right side of the cup’s body, curving upward and then downward in a smooth, C-shaped loop. The lines are not perfectly straight or symmetrical, suggesting a freehand sketch. There are no labels, colors, textures, or additional elements. The drawing is centered in the frame, with ample white space surrounding it. The object is clearly identifiable as a cup, and no bell or other shapes are present.
